title: Detection of Human Rhinovirus by PCR following Sanger Sequencing
author: Talita B Gagliardi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w83fhyn

steps.title: Prepare the PCR reaction using 3ul...
steps.instruction: Prepare the PCR reaction using 3ul of random cDNA; 10pM of the mixture of forward primers (B1, B2 and B3) and 10pM of the FR2 reverse primer.

steps.title: Run a Touch-down PCR following the...
steps.instruction: Run a Touch-down PCR following the cycling conditions suggested by the polymerase manufacturer although varying the annealing temperature from 68C to 52C (decreasing 2C after 2 cycles).

steps.title: Purify 5ul of the product from...
steps.instruction: Purify 5ul of the product from the first PCR using “ExoSAP-IT Express PCR Cleanup Reagent” (Thermo) and manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: Prepare again the PCR reaction using...
steps.instruction: Prepare again the PCR reaction using 3ul of purified amplicon; 10pM of the mixture of forward primers (B1, B2 and B3) and 10pM of the FR2 reverse primer.

steps.title: The PCR cycling conditions should be...
steps.instruction: The PCR cycling conditions should be set as suggested by the polymerase manufacturer, but this time the annealing temperature keeps set as 52C.

steps.title: Purify 10ul of the product from...
steps.instruction: Purify 10ul of the product from the second PCR using ExoSAP-IT and manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: Use this purified amplicon in a...
steps.instruction: Use this purified amplicon in a Sanger sequencing with the FR2 reverse primer only.

steps.title: Analyze the chromatogram and use the...
steps.instruction: Analyze the chromatogram and use the nucleotide sequence to search in a database as BLAST.
